title: Tachigali physophora
taxonomy: Tachigali physophora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. This classification places it within the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Tachigali. It is a member of the tribe Tachigaliaceae. The family Fabaceae is commonly known as the legume family, and it encompasses a large and diverse group of plants. Tachigali physophora is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.

intro: Tachigali physophora, also known as the "Brazilian iron tree," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its iron-rich wood and distinctive, reddish-brown color. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the ability to treat iron-deficiency anemia, as well as its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Tachigali physophora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Tachigali physophora is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including anemia, fever, and respiratory infections.
